title: Senior Director, Equipment and Engineering
description:
Main Responsibilities:

• Build a compliant and efficient equipment engineering team for the Singapore plant, including creating relevant job descriptions, recruiting, and training personnel.

• Manage the equipment engineering team at the Singapore site, coordinating work and communication within the team, and enhancing team members' technical and management skills.

• Develop and ensure the achievement of annual plans, goals, and budget for the department and the plant.

• Participate in the installation, commissioning, and acceptance of ongoing projects at the Singapore plant, approve execution results, and ensure smooth project delivery.

• Prepare for production readiness in the equipment engineering department, ensuring timely project delivery and smooth production processes.

• Ensure the availability and functionality of equipment, facilities, and power systems, conduct trial runs, validation, and calibration of new installations, properly use facilities and power equipment, and ensure timely maintenance, adjustment, and cleaning to guarantee operational safety and production needs.

• Review equipment, facilities, and power-related deviation investigations, take corrective actions, and implement preventive measures to avoid recurrence.

• Review change requests for equipment, facilities, and power systems, assess quality and safety impacts, and implement required measures to mitigate the impact of changes.

• Lead research and promotion of new technologies and processes in equipment and engineering projects, approve technical documents such as design changes and engineering work orders.

• Continuously optimize and improve the status, operability, safety, and reliability of production equipment, facilities, and power systems to ensure product quality and site safety, and improve production efficiency.

• Facilitate cross-site coordination for the equipment engineering department, incorporating technical and best practices from the Changzhou and Taixing sites in China into the Singapore plant, while ensuring compliance with local laws and regulations.

Competencies:

• Bachelor's degree or higher.

• Major in Mechanical, Electrical, or Chemical Automation, or related fields.

• Over fifteen years of engineering and maintenance management experience in pharmaceutical or chemical industries, with at least three years in a managerial role in an engineering department.

• Strong coordination and communication skills.

• Excellent problem-solving and analytical abilities.

• Proficient in reading and writing in both English and Chinese, capable of fluent technical communication with both the local Singapore team and the technical team in China.

• Proficient in using MS Office software.
